user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Implement 3-phase PWM motor control on TC387
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate 3-phase PWM on GTM TOM1 in center-aligned complementary mode at 20 kHz with 1 µs rising/falling dead time, with synchronous start and synchronous update enabled. Map phases (subject to TOUT verification) U=HS P02.0/LS P02.7, V=HS P02.1/LS P02.4, W=HS P02.2/LS P02.5; use complementary pairing (0/1, 2/3, 4/5) with low-side inverted and hardware dead-time via TOM1 DTM. Implement percentage duty (0..100) with initial U=25, V=50, W=75 and update step=10 using wrap rule. Provide initGtmTom3phInv() and updateGtmTom3phInvDuty(); add GTM enable/CMU clocks guard; add empty IfxGtm_periodEventFunction. Configure GTM ATOM0 CH0 ISR on CPU0 with priority 20 to toggle LED P13.0 at 2 Hz; main loop calls updateGtmTom3phInvDuty() then delays ~500 ms with STM ticks. TODO: Before deployment, verify and document final TOUT pin mapping and TOM1 channel indices against the official TC38x pin map and update the configuration accordingly.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use unified high-level IfxGtm_Pwm on TOM1 Cluster_1 with complementary channel pairs and DTM-configured dead time handled via the driver config. Guard GTM enable and CMU CLK enable before init. Enable synchronous start/update in the IfxGtm_Pwm configuration and update all three phases using IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDuty for percentage-based duty steps. Route TOUT pins using the TC38x LFBGA292/516 pin map after verifying P02.x mappings and adjust TOM1 channel indices accordingly. Drive a 2 Hz LED toggle using IfxGtm_Atom_Timer with an interrupt on ATOM0 CH0 (CPU0, prio 20), and use IfxStm to realize a ~500 ms delay in the main loop. For ISR testability, provide a weakly-linked ATOM0 CH0 handler and an overridable hook/callback to allow mocking in unit tests; isolate LED toggle logic behind this hook.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified high-level PWM driver supports multi-channel center-aligned complementary operation with synchronous start/update and dead-time handling via configuration, matching the 3-phase inverter requirement on TOM1. It simplifies setup compared to submodule-specific drivers.

runtime_function.name: updateGtmTom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Apply percentage-based duty updates to U, V, W with synchronous update enabled, using wrap rule and 10% step increments.

initGtmTom3phInv.reentrancy: Non-reentrant; call exactly once during system initialization.
initGtmTom3phInv.thread_safety: Not thread-safe; invoke before enabling interrupts and from a single core/context.
initGtmTom3phInv.error_handling: Returns a success/failure status; on failure the PWM remains disabled and no partial activation persists. Implementation should record a diagnostic error for retrieval by higher layers.
initGtmTom3phInv.usage_notes: Idempotency is not guaranteed; do not call concurrently with any other GTM configuration routines.

updateGtmTom3phInvDuty.reentrancy: Non-reentrant; if callable from multiple contexts (e.g., task and ISR), guard with a critical section or lock.
updateGtmTom3phInvDuty.thread_safety: Single-writer model recommended. If updates may occur from ISR and task, protect access (disable local interrupts or use a lightweight lock) to avoid concurrent GTM register updates.
updateGtmTom3phInvDuty.error_handling: Validates input range 0..100% per phase. On invalid input or uninitialized module, returns failure and performs no update; a diagnostic error should be recorded.
updateGtmTom3phInvDuty.usage_notes: Uses synchronous shadow transfer; updates are applied atomically across the three phases on the next trigger.

driver_changes: Timer/ISR: Add IfxGtm_Atom_Timer for 2 Hz LED toggle on ATOM0 CH0 (CPU0, prio 20)

api_changes.reason: Unified driver handles multi-channel complementary setup and sync start
Unified driver percentage duty update with synchronous shadow transfer
Added 2 Hz LED toggle via GTM ATOM timer interrupt

migration_notes.context_preserved: The unified IfxGtm_Pwm is configured for TOM1 center-aligned complementary PWM at 20 kHz with 1 µs dead time. Synchronous start and update are enabled in the driver configuration, and duty is maintained as 0..100% with the requested initialization and increment/wrap rule. The GTM enable and CMU clock guards are applied before PWM init. An empty IfxGtm_periodEventFunction stub is kept. The ATOM0 CH0 ISR toggles P13.0 at 2 Hz, and the main loop pattern remains update then ~500 ms delay. Note: Verify TOUT mappings for P02.0/1/2/4/5/7 against IfxGtm_PinMap_TC38x_LFBGA292/516 and adjust TOM1 channel indices (target Cluster_1) accordingly; the unified driver will route pins via its config.

PWM.rationale: Driver locked by refiner node (user explicit or engine-scored)
